query: South Africa 2026 municipal elections voter registration statistics IEC
answer:
I now have comprehensive data to compile the report. Here is my synthesis:

---

# South Africa 2026 Municipal Elections: Voter Registration Statistics & IEC Preparations

## Election Overview

The **2026 South African municipal elections** are scheduled for **4 November 2026**, as announced by President Cyril Ramaphosa on 30 April 2026. The elections will cover all 8 metropolitan municipalities, 205 local municipalities, and 44 district municipalities across South Africa's nine provinces. A total of **4,488 wards** and **23,292 voting districts** will be in operation.

## Voter Registration Statistics

### Total Registered Voters
- **As of December 2025**: **27.67 million** South Africans were registered to vote, according to the Electoral Commission of South Africa (IEC).
- The **largest voting bloc** was citizens aged **30–39 years old**.
- **55% of registered voters were female**.

### New Registrations in 2025
- From **January through September 2025**, over **305,200 new voters** registered.
- Over **60,700 existing voters** updated their registration details in the same period.
- By **April 2025**, the IEC announced that **258,838 new voters** had registered through its various platforms, with the **majority being young people**.

### Online Registration
- The IEC acknowledged the effectiveness of **online voter self-registration**, which was rolled out during the 2021 municipal elections.
- On **9 March 2026**, the IEC commenced a **nationwide push for online voter registration**, encouraging all eligible citizens to use its self-service portal to register, update details, and check voter status.
- The IEC also increased its registration drives and **civic education efforts at schools and universities**.

### Registration Weekends
- **Voter registration weekends** are scheduled for **June (20–21 June) and August 2026**.
- The IEC was recruiting and training **over 70,000 employees** to assist with the June 2026 registration weekend, as reported on 1 April 2026.
- The election logo and tagline — **"Get Up, Show Up, Vote"** — were unveiled at the Gallagher Convention Centre. IEC Chairperson **Mosotho Moepya** said both were designed to attract the youth vote.

## Comparison with Previous Elections

| Election | Registered Voters | Turnout |
|---|---|---|
| **2021 Municipal Elections** | ~26.2 million | ~46% (historically low) |
| **2024 National & Provincial Elections** | ~27.6 million | N/A |
| **2026 Municipal Elections (as of Dec 2025)** | **27.67 million** | TBD |

The registered voter count of 27.67 million represents an increase of approximately **1.47 million** compared to the 2021 municipal election voters' roll (~26.2 million).

## Ward Demarcation Changes

The Municipal Demarcation Board (MDB) handed over final ward delimitations to the IEC on **10 December 2025**. Key changes:
- Total wards increased by **20**, from **4,468 to 4,488**.
- Voting districts by province:

| Province | Total Districts | Change since 2021 |
|---|---|---|
| Eastern Cape | 703 | +7 |
| Free State | 311 | -8 |
| Gauteng | 526 | -3 |
| KwaZulu-Natal | 921 | +20 |
| Limpopo | 568 | 0 |
| Mpumalanga | 408 | +8 |
| North West | 402 | -1 |
| Northern Cape | 233 | +1 |
| Western Cape | 416 | +10 |
| **National Total** | **4,488** | **+20** |

## IEC Institutional Context
- **Chairperson**: Mosotho Moepya
- **Chief Electoral Officer**: Sy Mamabolo
- **Deputy Chief Electoral Officer**: Masego Shiburi
- **Budget**: R17.12 billion (2026/27)
- **Headquarters**: Election House, Centurion, Gauteng
- **508 political parties** registered to contest the 2026 elections (including 62 new parties since 2021)
- Of the 508 parties, **295 registered nationally** and **404 at the provincial level**
- Over **R355 million** in public funding was allocated to 20 political parties

## Voter Sentiment (Ipsos Research, March 2026)
- **38%** of South Africans thought their local government was doing a good job
- **61%** were somewhat or very interested in politics and elections
- **64%** of people intended to vote in the 2026 municipal election
- **66%** said casting their vote was important to them
- **63%** said parties should work together at the local government level

## Sources
- Wikipedia: "2026 South African municipal elections" (accessed 15 June 2026)
- IEC Voter Registration Statistics (via elections.org.za, data as of 31 December 2025)
- eNCA: "IEC Confirms 508 Political Parties Registered" (4 November 2025)
- SAnews: "All systems go for 2026/7 Local Government Elections" (4 November 2025)
- Mail & Guardian: "Electoral Commission sets out 2026/27 local election schedule" (10 December 2025)
- Daily Maverick: "IEC rules out e-voting for 2026 local government elections" (23 April 2025)
- EWN: "IEC to recruit over 70k staffers to oversee voter registration weekend" (1 April 2026)
- eNCA: "IEC Pushes Online Voter Registration Ahead Of 2026 Municipal Polls" (9 March 2026)
- Ipsos: "Nearly half of South Africans feel politically homeless ahead of local elections" (26 March 2026)
- SAnews: "Ward boundaries finalised ahead of local elections" (11 December 2025)
